fundamentals.name: Target Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Target Corporation operates as a general merchandise retailer in the United States. It offers apparel for women, men, young adults, kids, toddlers, and babies, as well as jewelry, accessories, and shoes; and beauty products, such as skin and bath care, cosmetics, hair care, oral care, deodorant, and shaving products. The company also provides food and beverage products comprising dry and perishable grocery, including snacks, candy, beverages, deli, bakery, meat, produce, and food service; electronics which includes video games and consoles, toys, sporting goods, entertainment, and luggage; bed and bath, home décor, school/office supplies, storage, small appliances, kitchenware, greeting cards, party supplies, furniture, lighting, home improvement, and seasonal merchandise; and household essentials, such as household cleaning, paper products, over-the-counter healthcare, vitamins and supplements, baby gear, and pet supplies. In addition, it sells merchandise through periodic design and creative partnerships, and shop-in-shop experience; and in-store amenities. The company sells its products through its stores; and digital channels, including Target.com. Target Corporation was incorporated in 1902 and is headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota.

judgment.summary: Target shows solid revenue growth and returns, but faces challenges with declining earnings and high debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates with EPS of $1.71 vs $1.46; next earnings date is 2026-08-19.
positives: Revenue growth 6.7% YoY indicates top-line resilience.
Return on equity 22.0% and return on assets 5.7% show effective use of capital.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $3.14B supports operations and dividends.
Dividend yield of 3.6% offers income to shareholders.
Forward P/E of 13.78x is attractive relative to trailing P/E of 16.20x.
negatives: Earnings growth -24.7% YoY and quarterly earnings growth -24.6% are concerning.
Debt/Equity ratio of 117.55 is high, indicating significant leverage.
Current ratio of 0.93 and quick ratio of 0.18 suggest potential short-term liquidity issues.
PEG ratio of 2.36 indicates that the stock may be overvalued relative to its earnings growth.

news.paragraph: Target (TGT) is garnering positive attention with DA Davidson raising its price target and analysts generally viewing its valuation favorably after a strong year of shareholder returns. Jim Cramer also expressed a cautiously optimistic view, stating "I Don’t Think It’s So Bad." While recent earnings growth has been negative, analyst estimates point to a rebound in the current quarter and fiscal year, suggesting a potential turnaround.
